meta.description: A living review of ophthalmic AI research across foundation models, visual grounding, PEFT, and hyperbolic methods.

papers.title: A foundation model for generalizable disease detection from retinal images
papers.summary: RETFound is the anchor ophthalmic foundation model paper of the last five years, emphasizing large-scale pretraining for transfer across retinal tasks.
papers.notes: Official paper link confirmed. Figure placeholder should be replaced with the model overview from the paper or a redrawn schematic.

papers.title: Development and Validation of a Multimodal Multitask Vision Foundation Model for Generalist Ophthalmic Artificial Intelligence
papers.summary: VisionFM frames ophthalmic AI as a multimodal, multitask foundation model problem and is one of the strongest generalist references in the field.
papers.notes: High-value source even though NEJM AI is outside the initial venue list.

papers.title: EyeFound: A Multimodal Generalist Foundation Model for Ophthalmic Imaging
papers.summary: EyeFound is a strong multimodal generalist candidate for ophthalmic imaging and belongs in any first-pass dataset for this space.
papers.notes: Track final publication venue and replace placeholder metadata once confirmed.

papers.title: UrFound: Towards Universal Retinal Foundation Models via Knowledge-Guided Masked Modeling
papers.summary: UrFound pushes toward a universal retinal foundation model with knowledge-guided masked modeling and is a useful bridge between pretraining and downstream adaptation.
papers.notes: Final venue should be verified before citing as archival publication.

papers.title: RetBench: Which Ophthalmic Foundation Model Performs Best and Why?
papers.summary: A benchmark-style slot reserved for comparing ophthalmic foundation models; useful as an overview node even before all metadata is finalized.
papers.notes: Placeholder entry. Replace with final official source once benchmark metadata is confirmed.
